name: Jira REST API
what_it_does: Atlassian's project and issue tracking REST API for creating, querying, and managing issues, sprints, projects, and workflows in Jira Cloud and Jira Data Center.
use_cases: Creating and triaging issues from AI agent workflows during incident response
Querying JQL (Jira Query Language) for sprint status, backlog, and bug counts
Automating issue transitions through workflow states (To Do → In Progress → Done)
Generating sprint summaries and engineering metrics from issue data
Syncing GitHub PRs, commit SHAs, and deployment data to Jira issues
not_for: Teams not using Jira (high migration barrier)
Simple to-do lists or lightweight task management
Workloads requiring more than 10 API requests/second (rate limits apply)
best_when: Your engineering team uses Jira and you need agents to interact with issue tracking, sprint planning, or workflow automation programmatically.
avoid_when: You're not a Jira customer, or your use case is simple task tracking that doesn't justify Jira's complexity.

auth.notes: Jira Cloud uses OAuth 2.0 (3LO) for user-facing apps or API tokens (HTTP Basic) for server-to-server. Jira Data Center uses Personal Access Tokens. OAuth scopes are granular (read:jira-user, write:jira-work, etc.).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: up to 10 users, 2GB storage, community support
pricing.notes: API access is available on all plans. Free plan supports up to 10 users with full API access. Atlassian Data Center requires separate licensing.

performance.rate_limits: Jira Cloud: varies by endpoint; typically 100-1000 requests/minute per user; burst limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include errorMessages array and errors object with field-level details. JQL errors are descriptive. Some errors lack context for automated handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Creating issues is not idempotent — retries create duplicates. Agents must check for existing issues using JQL search before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: JQL syntax errors are common and return 400 — agents generating JQL should validate syntax
Issue key format (PROJECT-123) varies per project — agents must know the project key
Custom fields have non-human-readable IDs (customfield_10001) — field ID discovery required before use
Rate limits are per user/IP and not clearly documented — unexpected 429s occur under load
Transitions require knowing the target state ID — must query available transitions before transitioning
Jira Cloud and Jira Data Center REST APIs differ significantly — confirm which you're targeting
Pagination: maxResults capped at 100 per page; large issue lists require multiple requests
